title: Problem running via systemd after upgrade from 1.4 to 1.6.4
body:
Hi everybody.

I'm using podman in CentOS 8, which was recently upgraded to the version 1.6.4. After the upgrade, I'm facing an issue when starting the container via Systemd with the detach option.

I'm using rootless containers and one of my systemd files are bellow:

```
[Unit]
Description=Site Lnes
[Service]
Restart=on-failure
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/rm -f /home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.pid /home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.cid
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --conmon-pidfile /home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.pid --cidfile /home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.cid -v /home/web/lnes/src:/var/www/html:Z -v /home/web/lnes/config/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:Z -v /home/web/lnes/config/phpcustom.ini:/etc/php5/conf.d/custom.ini:Z -v /home/web/lnes/config/php-fpm.conf:/etc/php5/fpm.d/www.conf:Z -p 2001:8080 --userns=keep-id -d iq/lnes
ExecStop=/usr/bin/sh -c "podman rm -f `cat /home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.cid`" ; rm -f /home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.cid
KillMode=none
Type=forking
PIDFile=/home/web/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/lnes/lnes.pid
User=web
Group=web
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

Everything was working correctly. But now, when running via systemd, I receive an error when starting and this log information:

```
Job for lnes.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl status lnes.service" and "journalctl -xe" for details.

Mar 03 15:43:04 messenger.iqm.unicamp.br podman[13187]: Error: error from slirp4netns while setting up port redirection: map[desc:bad request: add_hostfwd: slirp_add_hostfwd fa...
```

If I remove the -d (--detach) option from the systemd file, everything works perfectly, but I don't have the same result running podman run manually, which works both ways (with or without the -d option). The systemd method was working perfectly with the 1.4 version of podman.

```
[root@messenger andre.paiz]# systemctl --version
systemd 239
+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX +IMA -APPARMOR +SMACK +SYSVINIT +UTMP +LIBCRYPTSETUP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +ACL +XZ +LZ4 +SECCOMP +BLKID +ELFUTILS +KMOD +IDN2 -IDN +PCRE2 default-hierarchy=legacy
```

```
[root@messenger andre.paiz]# podman info --debug
```
